AI Financial Analyst — Parte 1: Data Layer

ai-finance
python
data
Costruiamo il primo modulo: scarico dati fondamentali con yfinance e li salviamo in locale.
Author

Simone Boesso

Published

May 26, 2026

Obiettivo

Il Data Layer è il fondamento del sistema. Il suo compito è recuperare bilanci, cash flow e prezzi storici per qualsiasi ticker e salvarli in locale per evitare rate limiting durante lo sviluppo.

Installazione

Code 
pip install yfinance pandas

Scaricare i dati base

Code 
import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd

TICKER = "AAPL"
ticker = yf.Ticker(TICKER)
info = ticker.info

print(f"Azienda:  {info['shortName']}")
print(f"Settore:  {info['sector']}")
print(f"Market Cap: ${info['marketCap']:,.0f}")
Azienda:  Apple Inc.
Settore:  Technology
Market Cap: $4,528,552,280,064

Income Statement

Code 
income = ticker.financials.T
income[["Total Revenue", "Net Income", "EBITDA"]].head()
Total Revenue Net Income EBITDA
2025-09-30 4.161610e+11 1.120100e+11 1.447480e+11
2024-09-30 3.910350e+11 9.373600e+10 1.346610e+11
2023-09-30 3.832850e+11 9.699500e+10 1.258200e+11
2022-09-30 3.943280e+11 9.980300e+10 1.305410e+11
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN

Balance Sheet

Code 
balance = ticker.balance_sheet.T
balance[["Total Assets", "Total Debt", "Stockholders Equity"]].head()
Total Assets Total Debt Stockholders Equity
2025-09-30 3.592410e+11 9.865700e+10 7.373300e+10
2024-09-30 3.649800e+11 1.066290e+11 5.695000e+10
2023-09-30 3.525830e+11 1.110880e+11 6.214600e+10
2022-09-30 3.527550e+11 1.324800e+11 5.067200e+10
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN

Cash Flow

Code 
cf = ticker.cashflow.T
cf[["Operating Cash Flow", "Free Cash Flow", "Capital Expenditure"]].head()
Operating Cash Flow Free Cash Flow Capital Expenditure
2025-09-30 1.114820e+11 9.876700e+10 -1.271500e+10
2024-09-30 1.182540e+11 1.088070e+11 -9.447000e+09
2023-09-30 1.105430e+11 9.958400e+10 -1.095900e+10
2022-09-30 1.221510e+11 1.114430e+11 -1.070800e+10
2021-09-30 NaN NaN NaN

Prezzi storici (5 anni)

Code 
prices = ticker.history(period="5y")
prices[["Close", "Volume"]].tail()
Close Volume
Date
2026-05-19 00:00:00-04:00 298.970001 42243600
2026-05-20 00:00:00-04:00 302.250000 38229800
2026-05-21 00:00:00-04:00 304.989990 42965100
2026-05-22 00:00:00-04:00 308.820007 43627900
2026-05-26 00:00:00-04:00 308.329987 41880194

Salvare in locale

Per non chiamare le API ad ogni render, salviamo tutto in parquet:

Code 
import os

os.makedirs("data", exist_ok=True)

income.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_income.parquet")
balance.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_balance.parquet")
cf.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_cashflow.parquet")
prices.to_parquet(f"data/{TICKER}_prices.parquet")

print("Dati salvati in data/")
Dati salvati in data/

Prossimi passi

Nel prossimo post useremo questi dati per calcolare le metriche fondamentali: ROE, ROIC, FCF yield, P/E, EV/EBITDA.
